quotes.text: An old friend of mine once told me, you know, just be yourself - unless you suck.
quotes.speaker: John Kennedy
quotes.context: Republican U.S. Senator from Louisiana, known for his folksy one-liners, sharing a piece of self-deprecating advice. He used the line in a Jan. 25, 2018 Fox News interview. Innocuous and funny, with no target or policy edge.

source.title: 15 times Sen. John Kennedy got folksy, snarky or both | nola.com

quotes.text: I make up different names for my cat all the time—Flapjack, Bowtie, Popcorn. But he's really, 'Hey you, cat.'
quotes.speaker: Christopher Walken
quotes.context: The actor on his pet cat in an Interview Magazine feature

source.title: Christopher Walken on Cats, Characters, and Career Highlights - Interview Magazine

quotes.text: Defense isn't about stopping the other team. It's about making them stop themselves. There's a real difference and most coaches never figure it out.
quotes.speaker: Gregg Popovich
quotes.context: Press conference following Game 3 of the 2014 NBA Finals, AT&T Center, San Antonio
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Pop's philosophy-over-X-and-Os framing, the quiet dismissal of lesser coaches, and the compressed, declarative sentences are pitch-perfect for him. The subtle tell is the second sentence — Pop would sooner imply that point than state it so directly.

quotes.text: I think people misunderstand what a riff actually is. It's not decoration — it's the argument. Everything else in the song is just evidence.
quotes.speaker: Jack White
quotes.context: Rolling Stone, 'The Gospel of Jack White' feature, October 2022
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Jack genuinely talks about guitar and songwriting in exactly this aphoristic, slightly combative way, and 'argument' as a musical metaphor is totally his register. The subtle giveaway is that he'd more likely say 'the question' than 'the argument' — he tends toward the Socratic, not the rhetorical.

quotes.text: I will do it myself where does he live?
quotes.speaker: Cory Booker
quotes.context: Cory Booker, then the Democratic Mayor of Newark, NJ, replied on Twitter when a resident said her elderly father needed his driveway shoveled during a major snowstorm. He showed up with volunteers within minutes.

source.title: Cory Booker Shovels Snow After Twitter Request (VIDEO) | HuffPost

quotes.text: The thing nobody tells you about being an astronaut is how much of the job is just waiting. You train for years to be ready for twelve minutes of chaos.
quotes.speaker: Scott Kelly
quotes.context: Interview on Lex Fridman Podcast, Episode 284, February 2022
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Scott Kelly is famously dry and plainspoken about the unglamorous realities of spaceflight, so the waiting-versus-chaos framing is completely in character. The giveaway is the specific 'twelve minutes,' which I chose to sound plausible — he'd likely leave the number vague.
